# feat: persist and list todos

Implement the first slice of the todo CLI.

## Acceptance criteria

- `TODO_DB=/path todo add "Buy milk"` persists a todo and prints `added 1`.
- Titles are trimmed; an empty or whitespace-only title exits non-zero, prints
  `title must not be empty` to stderr, and does not modify the database.
- IDs start at 1 and increase monotonically.
- `TODO_DB=/path todo list` prints one line per todo in ID order using exactly
  `<id>\t<status>\t<title>`; new todos have status `active`.
- State survives separate process invocations.
- Add behavior-level tests. Use only the Go standard library.

## Verification

- `gofmt -w .`
- `go test ./...`
- `go vet ./...`

- State survives separate process invocations.
- Add behavior-level tests. Use only the Go standard library.

issue.body:
# feat: persist and list todos

Implement the first slice of the todo CLI.

## Acceptance criteria

- `TODO_DB=/path todo add "Buy milk"` persists a todo and prints `added 1`.
- Titles are trimmed; an empty or whitespace-only title exits non-zero, prints
  `title must not be empty` to stderr, and does not modify the database.
- IDs start at 1 and increase monotonically.
- `TODO_DB=/path todo list` prints one line per todo in ID order using exactly
  `<id>\t<status>\t<title>`; new todos have status `active`.
- State survives separate process invocations.
- Add behavior-level tests. Use only the Go standard library.

## Verification

- `gofmt -w .`
- `go test ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
